title: Cognitive and behavioural correlates of psychosis in genetic subtypes of Prader-Willi syndrome.

assets.abstract: BACKGROUND: Prader-Willi Syndrome (PWS) is a rare neurodevelopmental disorder resulting from the loss of paternally expressed, maternally imprinted genes within the chromosome 15q11-q13 region. While core features such as hyperphagia and developmental delay are nearly universal, the maternal uniparental disomy (mUPD) subtype carries a significantly higher risk of psychosis compared to the paternal deletion subtype. This study investigated the neurocognitive and behavioural mechanisms driving this genotype-specific vulnerability within a predictive processing framework.
